prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `fe9372f3`.
codex_response:
Implemented `fe9372f3` end-to-end. The rule is: keep the red 5-cell plus, draw blue diagonal rays through its center to the borders, and extend the center row and column with the repeating `8,8,4` pattern outside the plus. The summary hint was consistent with the official examples, so I used it; no `notes.md` was needed, and no new shared primitive was added.

The task package is in [__init__.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_fe9372f3/__init__.py#L1), [verifier.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_fe9372f3/verifier.py#L1), and [generator.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_fe9372f3/generator.py#L1). The generator samples blank rectangular grids from `7..30` in each dimension and keeps the plus center at least 3 cells off every border, which matches the official task’s isolated-plus layout and produces the same family of ray lengths and stripe placements.

`conda run --no-capture-output -n arc python -m arc2.shared.verify --task fe9372f3` passed `3/3` official examples. `conda run --no-capture-output -n arc python -m arc2.shared.build --task fe9372f3 --n-examples 1000` succeeded and produced [task.json](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/fe9372f3/task.json), 1000 example files under [/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/fe9372f3/examples](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/fe9372f3/examples), and preview sheets [originals.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/fe9372f3/previews/originals.png), [preview_01.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/fe9372f3/previews/preview_01.png), [preview_02.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/fe9372f3/previews/preview_02.png), and [preview_03.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/fe9372f3/previews/preview_03.png). I opened `preview_01.png` and the generated family looks visually on-distribution.
